# @zakkster/lite-headless / toolbar

ARIA toolbar. A container of focusable controls with arrow-key
navigation, a single tab stop (roving tabindex), Home/End jump, and
optional separators between groups.

## What you get

```js
import { createToolbar } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/toolbar";

const tb = createToolbar({
    orientation: "horizontal",   // or "vertical"
    loop: true,                  // arrow keys wrap at the ends; default true
});

tb.attachRoot(rootEl);
tb.attachItem(boldBtn);
tb.attachItem(italicBtn);
tb.attachSeparator(sepEl);
tb.attachGroup(alignGroupEl);
tb.attachItem(alignLeftBtn);
tb.attachItem(alignCenterBtn);
tb.attachItem(alignRightBtn);
```

## Painted attributes

Painted on the root:
- `role="toolbar"`
- `data-toolbar-root`
- `data-orientation="horizontal|vertical"`
- `aria-orientation="vertical"` (only when vertical; W3C default is horizontal)

Painted on items:
- `data-toolbar-item`
- `tabindex="0"` for the current tab stop, `"-1"` for the rest
- `data-toolbar-item-focused` (the roving-focus highlight; usually
  matches DOM focus when using the default `STRATEGY_DOM_FOCUS`)
- `data-disabled` + `aria-disabled="true"` when disabled (set via
  `setItemDisabled` or marked declaratively before attach)

Painted on separators:
- `role="separator"`
- `data-toolbar-separator`
- `aria-orientation` PERPENDICULAR to the toolbar (vertical toolbar →
  horizontal separator, and vice versa, per WAI-ARIA APG)

Painted on groups:
- `role="group"`
- `data-toolbar-group`

## Keyboard

- **ArrowRight** / **ArrowDown** (matching orientation) — move to next non-disabled item
- **ArrowLeft** / **ArrowUp** — move to previous non-disabled item
- **Home** — first non-disabled item
- **End** — last non-disabled item

Disabled items are skipped during arrow nav (per ARIA APG). Loop is
on by default; pass `loop: false` to stop at the ends.

## Wrapper (`<lite-toolbar>`)

```html
<lite-toolbar orientation="horizontal" aria-label="Formatting">
    <button data-toolbar-item>Bold</button>
    <button data-toolbar-item>Italic</button>
    <div data-toolbar-separator></div>
    <div data-toolbar-group>
        <button data-toolbar-item>Left</button>
        <button data-toolbar-item>Center</button>
        <button data-toolbar-item>Right</button>
    </div>
</lite-toolbar>
```

Reactive attributes:
- `orientation="horizontal" | "vertical"` (read once on connect)
- `loop="false"` to disable wraparound

Host accessors:
- `host._toolbarInstance` (advanced)
- `host.setItemDisabled(el, bool)`
- `host.focusFirst()`, `host.focusLast()`

The wrapper uses `createRoleObserver` so late-injected items, separators,
and groups are auto-discovered without re-mounting.

## Composition

Toolbar items can be other primitives:

```html
<lite-toolbar>
    <lite-toggle-group data-toolbar-item>
        <button data-tg-item="left">L</button>
        <button data-tg-item="center">C</button>
        <button data-tg-item="right">R</button>
    </lite-toggle-group>
    <div data-toolbar-separator></div>
    <lite-menu data-toolbar-item>
        <button data-trigger>More...</button>
        <ul data-menu>...</ul>
    </lite-menu>
</lite-toolbar>
```

Each `data-toolbar-item` is one tab stop in the toolbar's roving
focus; the contained primitive owns its own interaction model
(toggle-group's roving among toggles, menu's open/close).

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. Booleans like `data-disabled` mirror the
W3C `aria-disabled`. Enums like `data-orientation` are documented in
the contract's Class 3 table.
